title: How to Win at the Sport of Business
author: Mark Cuban
subline: If you're prepared and you work harder than the next guy, you win.
customHTML:
<svg viewBox="0 0 1600 2400" preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid slice" style="position:absolute;inset:0;width:100%;height:100%">
  <!-- A half basketball court drawn in white hairlines occupies the bottom 2/3. The free-throw line is RED — the spot from which Cuban shoots his shot. -->
  <g transform="translate(800 1380)" fill="none">
    <rect x="-450" y="-150" width="900" height="500" stroke="#cccccc" stroke-width="2"/>
    <circle cx="0" cy="350" r="240" stroke="#cccccc" stroke-width="2"/>
    <circle cx="0" cy="350" r="60" stroke="#cccccc" stroke-width="2"/>
    <line x1="-150" y1="-150" x2="150" y2="-150" stroke="#cccccc" stroke-width="3"/>
    <rect x="-60" y="-150" width="120" height="20" fill="#cccccc"/>
    <ellipse cx="0" cy="-110" rx="55" ry="8" stroke="#cccccc" stroke-width="2"/>
    <path d="M -700 350 Q -700 -500 0 -500 Q 700 -500 700 350" stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="1"/>
    <line x1="-450" y1="350" x2="450" y2="350" stroke="#fc1c46" stroke-width="3"/>
    <circle cx="0" cy="350" r="10" fill="none" stroke="#fc1c46" stroke-width="2"/>
    <g font-family="'Sui','Söhne','Inter',system-ui,sans-serif" font-size="14" font-weight="500" fill="#4c4c4c" letter-spacing="0.2em">
      <text x="-700" y="-500" text-anchor="start">HOME</text>
      <text x="700" y="-500" text-anchor="end">AWAY</text>
    </g>
  </g>
</svg>
rationale: Cuban's frame: business IS a sport. Cover is literally a half basketball court drawn in hairlines — backboard, key, free-throw circle, three-point arc. The free-throw line is the sole red element, a thick 3px stroke, the spot from which he takes the shot. Title sits in a 3-line stack at the top, weight 500 command, mixed-case. The subtitle delivers the Cuban thesis: preparation + effort = winning.
